content.problemStatement:
Chef has opened a new ice cream parlor. His friend Chefina is going to visit the parlor very soon. Chef knows that Chefina is going to order a triple sundae on her visit. However, he does not know which flavors she would order.

Chef has **three** storage containers with capacities X,YX, YX,Y and ZZZ respectively. Each container can store **multiple** flavors of ice cream. Since Chef does not know which flavors Chefina would order, he wants to store **maximum number** of flavors in the storage containers.

Chef has a total of NNN flavors. In order to store the ithi^{th}ith flavor, Chef must select **exactly one** container and fill it with CiC_iCi​ amount of flavor iii.

Note that the total amount of ice cream filled in a container should not exceed its capacity.

Find the **maximum number** of flavors Chef can store in the containers.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains four integers N,X,YN,X,YN,X,Y and ZZZ.
The next line contains NNN integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes CiC_iCi​.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the maximum number of distinct flavors Chef can store using the three containers.
constraints: Sum of NNN overall test cases does not exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:

Store flavor {4}\{ 4 \}{4} in container XXX, since 10≤∣X∣10 \leq |X|10≤∣X∣.
Store flavors {1,3}\{ 1,3 \}{1,3} in container YYY, since (11+5)=16≤∣Y∣(11+5) = 16 \leq |Y|(11+5)=16≤∣Y∣.
Store flavors {5}\{ 5 \}{5} in container ZZZ, since 9≤∣Z∣9 \leq |Z|9≤∣Z∣.
This way we can store 444 flavors.

There can be other ways to store 444 flavours but we cannot store more than 444 flavours.

Explanation:
Test Case 222:

Store flavor {4}\{ 4 \}{4} in container XXX, since 4≤∣X∣=44 \leq |X| = 44≤∣X∣=4.
Store flavors {2,3}\{ 2,3 \}{2,3} in container YYY, since 2+3=5≤∣Y∣=52+3 = 5 \leq |Y| = 52+3=5≤∣Y∣=5.
Store flavors {5}\{ 5 \}{5} in container ZZZ, since 5≤∣Z∣=55 \leq |Z|=55≤∣Z∣=5.
This way we can store 444 flavors.

It can be proven that we cannot store more than 444 flavors in this case.
